home.tagline: Realtime Voice AI for UI Navigation & Function Execution

mic.activeDetail: Voice session active. Speak now or tap to stop.
mic.missingKey: Set OPENAI_API_KEY in backend or provide an OpenAI API key.
mic.frontendKeyDisabled: Backend does not allow API keys from frontend. Set NAVAI_ALLOW_FRONTEND_API_KEY=true.
mic.clientSecretRejected: OpenAI rejected client_secret. Check API key and realtime model.
mic.backendOffline: No internet connection. Check your network.
mic.backendUnreachable: Could not connect to the NAVAI backend.
mic.backendOfflineStartBlocked: No internet connection. Reconnect to the network to start NAVAI.

teleprompterLines: Say: 'Take me to Documentation' to open setup guides and libraries.
Say: 'Go to Start implementation' to review the service and activation steps.
Ask: 'What does this page do?' to get context for the current screen.
Use voice scrolling: 'Scroll to the bottom' or 'Back to top'.
Ask for specific actions: 'Search voice-frontend in the documentation'.

docsPage.title: 📚 NAVAI Voice Runtime Docs
docsPage.description: 🧠 Technical summary extracted from the official Luxisoft/NAVAI repository: architecture, HTTP contract, runtime flow, and environment setup.

sections.title: What NAVAI provides
sections.description: NAVAI is a voice-first runtime that lets an AI agent navigate app UI and execute allowed frontend/backend functions in real time.
bullets: Core packages: @navai/voice-backend, @navai/voice-frontend, @navai/voice-mobile.
Backend mints secure ephemeral client_secret for OpenAI Realtime.
Frontend runtime resolves routes and executes safe tool calls.

sections.title: Backend HTTP contract
sections.description: Official routes from backend integration:
bullets: POST /navai/realtime/client-secret
GET /navai/functions
POST /navai/functions/execute
If backend has OPENAI_API_KEY, server key always has priority over request apiKey.

sections.title: Realtime flow
sections.description: Documented runtime sequence used by playground integrations:
bullets: 1) Client requests ephemeral secret from backend.
2) Client fetches backend function registry.
3) Agent is built with navigate_to + execute_app_function tools.
4) RealtimeSession connects and executes navigation/function calls.

sections.title: Key environment variables
sections.description: Most relevant variables from official docs:
bullets: OPENAI_API_KEY, OPENAI_REALTIME_MODEL, OPENAI_REALTIME_VOICE
OPENAI_REALTIME_INSTRUCTIONS, OPENAI_REALTIME_LANGUAGE
NAVAI_FUNCTIONS_FOLDERS, NAVAI_API_URL, NAVAI_ALLOW_FRONTEND_API_KEY

sections.title: Quick start commands
sections.description: Reference setup commands extracted from repository guides.
sections.code:
npm install @navai/voice-backend @navai/voice-frontend @openai/agents zod
npx navai-setup-voice-frontend
navai-generate-web-loaders

sections.title: Official source links
sections.description: Primary sources used for this page:
bullets: https://github.com/Luxisoft/navai
packages/voice-backend/README.md
packages/voice-frontend/README.md
packages/voice-mobile/README.md

implementationPage.title: Run NAVAI implementation
implementationPage.description: We implement voice navigation and function execution in your site/app with production-ready configuration.

sections.title: What's included?
sections.description: Delivery scope for each implementation:
bullets: Integration of the NAVAI backend contract and the frontend runtime.
Mapping of routes and synonyms for your application structure.
Validation, quality-control checklist, and go-live support.

sections.title: Delivery process
sections.description: Typical implementation process:
bullets: Discovery call + route/function inventory.
Technical implementation in a staging environment.
Acceptance testing and production launch.

sections.title: Pricing model
sections.description: Pricing depends on the number of menus/routes, the complexity of the navigation logic, and the execution depth required for the tool.

plans.name: Growth
highlights: Expanded route map and synonym coverage

plans.name: Scale
highlights: Complex navigation trees and fallback strategy

sections.title: Step 1 Create the project
sections.description: Create a minimal Express service, enable ESM, and prepare the folder that NAVAI will scan for backend functions.
bullets: Use Node.js 20 or newer.
This structure already works for local demos, VPS, or containers.

codeBlocks.label: Terminal
codeBlocks.code:
mkdir navai-api-demo
cd navai-api-demo
npm init -y
npm pkg set type=module
npm pkg set scripts.dev="node --watch src/server.js"
npm pkg set scripts.start="node src/server.js"
mkdir src
mkdir src/ai
mkdir src/ai/functions-modules

sections.title: Step 2 Install dependencies
sections.description: Install the four packages required to expose the NAVAI HTTP contract on the first attempt.

sections.title: Step 3 Configure environment variables
sections.description: Create a `.env` file with the minimum values for OpenAI Realtime and the folder where NAVAI will load backend functions.

codeBlocks.label: .env
codeBlocks.code:
PORT=3000
CORS_ORIGIN=http://localhost:4321
OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-...
OPENAI_REALTIME_MODEL=gpt-realtime
OPENAI_REALTIME_VOICE=marin
OPENAI_REALTIME_INSTRUCTIONS=You are a helpful assistant for UI navigation and function execution.
OPENAI_REALTIME_LANGUAGE=English
OPENAI_REALTIME_CLIENT_SECRET_TTL=600
NAVAI_FUNCTIONS_FOLDERS=src/ai/functions-modules
NAVAI_ALLOW_FRONTEND_API_KEY=false

sections.title: Step 4 Create a backend function
sections.description: Add one callable export first so `GET /navai/functions` and `POST /navai/functions/execute` prove the backend is really ready.
bullets: NAVAI converts `getCompanySummary` into `get_company_summary` automatically.
The function can return plain JSON and does not need extra wrappers.

codeBlocks.label: src/ai/functions-modules/getCompanySummary.js

sections.title: Step 5 Create the NAVAI server
sections.description: Mount your health route first and then register NAVAI routes in the same Express app with the environment values you configured above.
bullets: This exposes the full NAVAI contract on the same API.
You can keep your own business routes in the same Express app.

codeBlocks.label: src/server.js

sections.title: Step 6 Test the API
sections.description: Run these checks in order. If all of them pass, the backend is ready for Web, Mobile, Astro, Vue, Laravel, or any client that consumes the HTTP contract.
bullets: Start the server once before using `curl`.
If `client-secret` fails, review `OPENAI_API_KEY` first.

sections.title: Step 7 Connect Web or Mobile
sections.description: Once the API is alive, the client only needs the backend base URL and the three NAVAI endpoints already exposed by Express.
bullets: Web and Mobile request `client_secret` from `/navai/realtime/client-secret`.
They discover tools through `/navai/functions` and execute remote tools through `/navai/functions/execute`.
If your frontend runs on another origin, add it to `CORS_ORIGIN` before testing the voice session.

tabsSection.title: Choose your base stack
tabsSection.description: Pick the route that matches your backend. Every step below updates to the selected stack so you can follow one path from project creation to the first NAVAI request.
bullets: Use Node.js + Express if you want the quickest first deployment.
Switch tabs before copying files so the commands and file names stay aligned.

tabs.label: Node.js
tabs.title: Node.js + Express
tabs.description: Fastest path for a clean NAVAI API from zero using plain JavaScript and no build step.
bullets: Recommended for the first successful integration attempt.
Good when NAVAI will run as a dedicated backend service.

sections.title: Step 1 Create the project
sections.description: Create a minimal Express service, enable ESM, and prepare the folder that NAVAI will scan for backend functions.
bullets: Use Node.js 20 or newer.
This structure already works for local demos, VPS, or containers.

codeBlocks.label: Terminal
codeBlocks.code:
mkdir navai-api-demo
cd navai-api-demo
npm init -y
npm pkg set type=module
npm pkg set scripts.dev="node --watch src/server.js"
npm pkg set scripts.start="node src/server.js"
mkdir src
mkdir src/ai
mkdir src/ai/functions-modules

sections.title: Step 2 Install dependencies
sections.description: Install the four packages required to expose the NAVAI HTTP contract on the first attempt.

sections.title: Step 3 Configure environment variables
sections.description: Create a `.env` file with the minimum values for OpenAI Realtime and the folder where NAVAI will load backend functions.

codeBlocks.label: .env
codeBlocks.code:
PORT=3000
CORS_ORIGIN=http://localhost:4321
OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-...
OPENAI_REALTIME_MODEL=gpt-realtime
OPENAI_REALTIME_VOICE=marin
OPENAI_REALTIME_INSTRUCTIONS=You are a helpful assistant for UI navigation and function execution.
OPENAI_REALTIME_LANGUAGE=English
OPENAI_REALTIME_CLIENT_SECRET_TTL=600
NAVAI_FUNCTIONS_FOLDERS=src/ai/functions-modules
NAVAI_ALLOW_FRONTEND_API_KEY=false

sections.title: Step 4 Create a backend function
sections.description: Add one callable export first so `GET /navai/functions` and `POST /navai/functions/execute` prove the backend is really ready.
bullets: NAVAI converts `getCompanySummary` into `get_company_summary` automatically.
The function can return plain JSON and does not need extra wrappers.

codeBlocks.label: src/ai/functions-modules/getCompanySummary.js

sections.title: Step 5 Create the NAVAI server
sections.description: Mount your health route first and then register NAVAI routes in the same Express app with the environment values you configured above.
bullets: This exposes the full NAVAI contract on the same API.
You can keep your own business routes in the same Express app.

codeBlocks.label: src/server.js

sections.title: Step 6 Test the API
sections.description: Run these checks in order. If all of them pass, the backend is ready for Web, Mobile, Astro, Vue, Laravel, or any client that consumes the HTTP contract.
bullets: Start the server once before using `curl`.
If `client-secret` fails, review `OPENAI_API_KEY` first.

sections.title: Step 7 Connect Web or Mobile
sections.description: Once the API is alive, the client only needs the backend base URL and the three NAVAI endpoints already exposed by Express.
bullets: Web and Mobile request `client_secret` from `/navai/realtime/client-secret`.
They discover tools through `/navai/functions` and execute remote tools through `/navai/functions/execute`.
If your frontend runs on another origin, add it to `CORS_ORIGIN` before testing the voice session.

tabs.label: TypeScript
tabs.title: Express + TypeScript
tabs.description: Same quick route, but with typed files, `tsx` for local dev, and a build output for deployment.
bullets: Recommended if your team already keeps backend code typed.
The NAVAI contract stays the same, only the project setup changes.

sections.title: Step 1 Create the TypeScript project
sections.description: Create the project, prepare `src`, and add a minimal `tsconfig.json` so you can run locally with `tsx` and deploy from `dist` later.
bullets: Use Node.js 20 or newer.
This path keeps local setup fast and still gives you a production build.

codeBlocks.label: Terminal
codeBlocks.code:
mkdir navai-api-demo
cd navai-api-demo
npm init -y
npm pkg set type=module
npm pkg set scripts.dev="tsx watch src/server.ts"
npm pkg set scripts.build="tsc -p tsconfig.json"
npm pkg set scripts.start="node dist/server.js"
mkdir src
mkdir src/ai
mkdir src/ai/functions-modules

sections.title: Step 2 Install runtime and TypeScript tooling
sections.description: Install the same NAVAI runtime packages plus the minimum TypeScript toolchain for local execution and build output.
bullets: `tsx` lets you validate the integration without a manual compile step.
You can keep the same folder structure from the Node.js route.

codeBlocks.label: Terminal
codeBlocks.code:
npm install express cors dotenv @navai/voice-backend
npm install -D typescript tsx @types/cors @types/express @types/node

sections.title: Step 3 Configure environment variables
sections.description: Create a `.env` file with the same minimum NAVAI values. The TypeScript route uses the same backend contract and the same functions folder.
bullets: `OPENAI_API_KEY` must stay server-side only.
Leave `NAVAI_FUNCTIONS_FOLDERS=src/ai/functions-modules` so the server can find your tools.

codeBlocks.label: .env
codeBlocks.code:
PORT=3000
CORS_ORIGIN=http://localhost:4321
OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-...
OPENAI_REALTIME_MODEL=gpt-realtime
OPENAI_REALTIME_VOICE=marin
OPENAI_REALTIME_INSTRUCTIONS=You are a helpful assistant for UI navigation and function execution.
OPENAI_REALTIME_LANGUAGE=English
OPENAI_REALTIME_CLIENT_SECRET_TTL=600
NAVAI_FUNCTIONS_FOLDERS=src/ai/functions-modules
NAVAI_ALLOW_FRONTEND_API_KEY=false

sections.title: Step 4 Create the typed backend function
sections.description: Add one typed tool first. This keeps the payload shape explicit and gives you the same quick verification as the JavaScript route.
bullets: NAVAI still exposes the tool as `get_company_summary`.
You can keep returning plain JSON objects.

codeBlocks.label: src/ai/functions-modules/getCompanySummary.ts

sections.title: Step 5 Create `server.ts` with NAVAI
sections.description: Mount the health route and register NAVAI in the same Express app. The TypeScript version keeps the exact same HTTP endpoints as the Node.js route.
bullets: Your frontend should not notice any contract difference.
For production, build once and run `node dist/server.js`.

codeBlocks.label: src/server.ts

sections.title: Step 6 Run and verify the API
sections.description: Start the server with `tsx` and validate the health route, realtime secret, tool discovery, and one tool execution in that order.
bullets: For local testing, `npm run dev` is enough.
Before deployment, use `npm run build` and then `npm run start`.

sections.title: Step 7 Connect Web or Mobile
sections.description: Once the typed backend is alive, the client integration stays exactly the same because the NAVAI HTTP contract did not change.
bullets: Point the client to your TypeScript backend base URL.
Keep `/navai/realtime/client-secret`, `/navai/functions`, and `/navai/functions/execute` reachable to the app.
If your frontend runs on another origin, add it to `CORS_ORIGIN` before testing the voice session.

tabs.label: NestJS
tabs.title: NestJS + Mounted NAVAI Router
tabs.description: Best when you already have a Nest backend and want NAVAI under `/navai` without creating another API contract.
bullets: Use this route only if NestJS is already your real backend base.
NAVAI stays isolated inside its own mounted Express router.

sections.title: Step 1 Create the NestJS project
sections.description: Generate a fresh Nest app and create the folder where NAVAI will load backend functions. This keeps the example aligned with a real Nest backend from the start.
bullets: The command avoids the interactive wizard and leaves the project ready faster.
If you already have a Nest app, only create `src/ai/functions-modules`.

codeBlocks.label: Terminal
codeBlocks.code:
npx @nestjs/cli@latest new navai-api-demo --package-manager npm --skip-git
cd navai-api-demo
mkdir src/ai
mkdir src/ai/functions-modules

sections.title: Step 2 Install NAVAI dependencies
sections.description: Install the NAVAI backend package, dotenv, and an explicit `express` dependency so the mounted router is declared in your project.
bullets: This keeps the integration explicit even if Nest already uses Express internally.
You do not need a second HTTP server for this quick example.

codeBlocks.label: Terminal
codeBlocks.code:
npm install express dotenv @navai/voice-backend
npm install -D @types/express

sections.title: Step 3 Configure environment variables
sections.description: Create a `.env` file with the minimum realtime values and the folder that contains your NAVAI backend tools.
bullets: The same variables work for NestJS because the NAVAI contract is still the same.
Keep `NAVAI_ALLOW_FRONTEND_API_KEY=false` unless you have a very specific internal need.

codeBlocks.label: .env
codeBlocks.code:
PORT=3000
CORS_ORIGIN=http://localhost:4321
OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-...
OPENAI_REALTIME_MODEL=gpt-realtime
OPENAI_REALTIME_VOICE=marin
OPENAI_REALTIME_INSTRUCTIONS=You are a helpful assistant for UI navigation and function execution.
OPENAI_REALTIME_LANGUAGE=English
OPENAI_REALTIME_CLIENT_SECRET_TTL=600
NAVAI_FUNCTIONS_FOLDERS=src/ai/functions-modules
NAVAI_ALLOW_FRONTEND_API_KEY=false

sections.title: Step 4 Create the backend function module
sections.description: Add one typed tool inside `src/ai/functions-modules` so the mounted NAVAI router can discover and execute it from NestJS.
bullets: The exported function name is still converted into `snake_case` for the HTTP contract.
Start with one simple tool before adding database or service injections around it.

codeBlocks.label: src/ai/functions-modules/getCompanySummary.ts

sections.title: Step 5 Mount NAVAI inside NestJS
sections.description: Update `main.ts` to enable CORS, expose `/health`, and mount the NAVAI router under `/navai` inside the same Nest application.
bullets: Your existing Nest controllers can continue working as usual.
This keeps one backend URL for both your business API and NAVAI.

codeBlocks.label: src/main.ts

sections.title: Step 6 Run and verify the mounted routes
sections.description: Start the Nest server and validate the same four checks. If they respond well, the mounted router is ready for clients.
bullets: The checks are identical because NAVAI exposes the same endpoints under `/navai`.
If `GET /navai/functions` is empty, review `NAVAI_FUNCTIONS_FOLDERS` and the file export name first.

sections.title: Step 7 Connect Web or Mobile
sections.description: With NestJS already exposing `/navai`, the frontend only needs the base URL of the same backend and the standard NAVAI endpoints.
bullets: Point the app to the Nest backend base URL.
The NAVAI endpoints remain `/navai/realtime/client-secret`, `/navai/functions`, and `/navai/functions/execute`.
If the frontend runs on another origin, keep that origin in `CORS_ORIGIN` before testing the session.

panelPage.publicExperienceRulesValidationRule: The organizer may request identity, contact, or payment validation before delivering the reward and may void participations involving fraud or rule violations.
panelPage.publicExperienceRulesLegalAcceptanceRule: By participating you accept the terms, privacy policies, data processing rules, AI usage notices, and other legal conditions linked in this experience.
panelPage.publicExperienceCommentPlaceholder: Share your experience or leave a note...
panelPage.publicExperienceCommentFilteredEmptyMess: There are no comments with that rating.

panelPage.publicExperienceEntryTimeDescription: Make sure you have enough time to respond calmly and without interruptions.
panelPage.publicExperienceEntryNoiseDescription: Use a low-noise space so your voice can be heard clearly.
panelPage.publicExperienceEntryVideoDescription: Video recording may be requested for security and process validation.
panelPage.publicExperienceEntryBotTitle: Do not use bots or misuse
panelPage.publicExperienceEntryBotDescription: Do not use automations, impersonation, or practices that alter the platform.
panelPage.publicExperienceEntryTermsDescription: You must comply with the usage, privacy, and security rules defined for this experience.
panelPage.publicExperienceEntryTermsCheckboxLabel: I have read the warnings and accept the terms and conditions to continue.

panelPage.userProfileDescription: Update your personal, professional, contact, and social information to display it in public experiences.
panelPage.userAccountDeletionSectionDescription: Confirm if you want to move your account into the deletion period. Information is preserved for traceability and the account is deactivated after 5 days.
panelPage.userAccountDeletionKeepRecordsNotice: The account is not physically deleted and records are not removed. Traceability is preserved during this period and, once inactive, you will no longer be able to sign in or expose public information linked to your user.
panelPage.userAccountDeletionDialogDescription: If you confirm, your account enters the deletion period today. After 5 days it will be deactivated, you will no longer be able to sign in, and your public information will stop being displayed. Records are not deleted to preserve traceability.
panelPage.userAccountDeletionRequestSuccessMessage: The account entered the deletion period. It will be automatically deactivated in 5 days.
panelPage.userVerificationSectionDescription: Upload your face image and both sides of your identity document to request a manual account verification.
panelPage.userVerificationCurrentStatusDescription: Check the current status, submission date, and latest response from the review team.
panelPage.userVerificationRequirementsDescription: Make sure the images are clear and the information matches your identity document.
panelPage.userVerificationRequirementFaceImage: Include a clear image of your face.
panelPage.userVerificationRequirementFrontImage: Upload the front side of the identity document.
panelPage.userVerificationRequirementBackImage: Upload the back side of the identity document.
panelPage.userVerificationRequirementClearData: Verify that the full name and document number are easy to read.
panelPage.userVerificationSelfieDescription: Upload a recent image where your face is fully visible.
panelPage.userVerificationFrontDescription: Upload the front side of the identity document.
panelPage.userVerificationBackDescription: Upload the back side of the identity document.
panelPage.userVerificationSubmitSuccessMessage: The verification request was submitted successfully.
panelPage.userVerificationSubmitErrorMessage: The verification request could not be submitted.
panelPage.userVerificationImageUploadSuccessMessag: The image was uploaded successfully to Cloudflare.
panelPage.userVerificationImageUploadErrorMessage: The verification image could not be uploaded.
panelPage.userVerificationImageTypeErrorMessage: Only image files can be uploaded for verification.
panelPage.userVerificationImageSizeErrorMessage: Each verification image must be smaller than 10 MB.
panelPage.userManagementDescription: Manage users, roles, and operational permissions to edit or delete panel data.
panelPage.userManagementUsersSectionDescription: Assign the operational role for each authenticated user who has already entered the panel.
panelPage.userManagementRolesSectionDescription: Define whether each role can edit tables, delete records, or manage users.
panelPage.userManagementVerificationsSectionDescri: Review identity requests from the oldest pending request to the newest one.
panelPage.userManagementUsersEmptyMessage: There are no panel users yet.

panelPage.userManagementVerificationSavedMessage: The verification request was updated successfully.
panelPage.userManagementVerificationReviewPanelDes: Change the verification status and send a response that the user will see in their profile.

panelPage.paymentsDescription: Buy NAVAI entries and review related purchases, credits, and accounting records.
panelPage.plusPlanSummary: Buy single entries to participate in evaluations and surveys across the platform.
panelPage.plusPlanFeatureTwo: Fixed price of COP 19,900 with VAT included.
panelPage.plusPlanFeatureThree: Entries are consumed when you start an evaluation or survey.
panelPage.plusPendingOrderDescription: If you already opened Wompi, you can resume checkout or confirm the purchase manually when you return.
panelPage.plusConfirmSuccessMessage: The payment was confirmed and 1 entry was credited.
panelPage.plusCreateOrderErrorMessage: Could not create the entry purchase order.
panelPage.plusConfirmErrorMessage: Could not confirm the entry payment.
panelPage.plusOrdersEmptyMessage: No purchases have been recorded yet.
panelPage.dailyAttemptLimitHelpText: Define how many times per day the same user can answer this evaluation or survey.
panelPage.publicExperienceDailyAttemptLimitReached: You already reached the daily limit allowed for this evaluation or survey.
panelPage.publicExperiencePlusRequiredMessage: This experience requires at least 1 available entry.
panelPage.publicExperienceNonPlusOnlyMessage: This experience is only available to users without NAVAI Plus.
panelPage.publicExperienceMissingScheduleMessage: This experience does not have a configured start or end date.
panelPage.publicExperienceReactivationPendingLabel: Reactivation depends on the current restriction.
panelPage.referralsDescription: Share your invite link, generate a QR code, and review referral traceability and entries.
panelPage.plusReferralDetectedDescription: This 1-entry purchase will use the referral code captured in this browser.
panelPage.referralAttributionAcceptedMessage: The referral was attached to the entry purchase.
panelPage.referralAttributionInvalidMessage: The referral code is invalid. The purchase will continue without referral attribution.
panelPage.referralAttributionSelfMessage: You cannot use your own referral code.
panelPage.referralAttributionAlreadyAssignedMessag: Your account already has a referral assigned.
panelPage.referralAttributionIneligibleMessage: This account already purchased an entry and is not eligible for the referral reward.
panelPage.referralProgramRuleOne: Each approved referral that buys 1 entry adds 3 entries.
panelPage.referralProgramRuleTwo: Referral rewards are added as available entries for evaluations and surveys.
panelPage.referralProgramRuleThree: Traceability records purchases, rewards, and entry consumption.
panelPage.referralProgramCodeCardDescription: Share this code or link to invite new users.
panelPage.referralProgramShareDescription: Use the link or QR code to invite users. When they buy 1 entry, you receive 3 entries.
panelPage.referralProgramQrDescription: Scan or share this QR to open the invitation link.
panelPage.referralsTableDescription: Tracking of invited users and related entry purchases.
panelPage.referralLedgerDescription: Record of granted rewards and entry consumption.
panelPage.referralLedgerEmptyMessage: There are no referral movements yet.
panelPage.referralLoadErrorMessage: Could not load the referral program.
panelPage.userManagementRoutesSectionDescription: Define which roles, including visitors without an active session, can open each page or public route.
panelPage.userManagementRoutesEmptyMessage: There are no route access rules configured yet.
panelPage.pointsDescription: Request PT point cashouts to real money and track each request status.
panelPage.withdrawalsDescription: Manage point cashout withdrawal requests and their payout status.
panelPage.pointsPaymentSettingsDescription: Set the account where you want to receive money when submitting a points cashout.

panelPage.pointsRequestsSectionDescription: Track status and response for each request.

panelPage.pointsRequestPointsInvalidMessage: Enter a valid PT value greater than zero.
panelPage.pointsTraceabilityDescription: Point movement history related to cashout requests and adjustments.
panelPage.pointsTraceabilityEmptyMessage: There are no traceability movements to display yet.
panelPage.pointsWalletUsageDescription: Submit PT-to-money cashout requests, set withdrawal details, and track payout status from this screen.
panelPage.pointsWalletUsageEntryNote: If you converted PT to money, you can use that money in your digital wallet to buy entries in the Entries section.
panelPage.withdrawalsRequestsDescription: Review point cashout requests, validate payment details, and update payout status.

panelPage.entryPackagesDescription: Manage entry packages with dynamic USD pricing and VAT.

panelPage.entryPackagesEditorDescription: Configure USD price, VAT included, and number of entries.
panelPage.entryPackagesPurchaseDescription: Select a package to buy entries with dynamic pricing (USD + VAT).
